#  This is the template for Windsond output configuration files
#  Each copy of this file describes how Windsond generates a specific data format.
#  The name of the configuration file determines the naming of generated files.

#  Configuration files are named with the pattern "fileformat_<XXX>.txt".
#  Generated files will then be named on the pattern <sounding>.<XXX>,
#  where <XXX> is the text used in the configuration file name.
#  <XXX> can include a file prefix. Example:
#  'fileformat_summary.csv.txt' will generate files like
#  '2019-12-31_0900.summary.csv'.

# Configuration files must be placed in the configured data file location.
# By default this location is My Documents/Windsond files/
#
# Any line that starts with '#' is ignored (which is why Windsond
# doesn't try to read these instructions).
# To enable a setting, remove the '#' in front of it.
# The wording must stay exact, or Windsond will be unable to understand the line

# A description of the file format, to show in the Settings dialog:
description=Template of CSV file format

# If enabled, new soundings generate a CSV file of this format,
# unless disabled in the Settings dialog:
#default_active

######################################################################
#  Enable one of these to control how data rows are selected.
#  Only one can be enabled.
#  'use_actual_reports' controls the major type of data format -- if
#  reports are used from the sonde at the altitudes the sonde happen to
#  be at.
#  If use_actual_reports is enabled, averaging between
#  multiple data points will not be done.
#use_actual_reports

#  The set of specific altitudes to report:
#  (change the list if you wish)
#altitude_agl_m=10 50 100

#  If altitude_agl_m or altitude_agl_ft is defined,
#  altitude_agl_spacing_m is only used for any higher altitudes:
#altitude_agl_spacing_m=100

#  Works as altitude_agl_m, but uses feet as altitude unit:
altitude_agl_ft=50 100 200 300

#  If altitude_agl_m or altitude_agl_ft is defined,
#  altitude_agl_spacing_ft is only used for any higher altitudes:
altitude_agl_spacing_ft=100

######################################################################
# Controls what data is selected:
remove_data_before_gps_fix
#remove_data_before_take_off
#remove_data_after_first_landing
#remove_data_after_peak
#remove_data_at_recurring_altitudes

######################################################################
# Controls how the data columns are separated in the file:
#column_separation=comma
#column_separation=tab
#column_separation=semicolon
column_separation=fixed-width

######################################################################
# Select parameters to report and their order
# (As usual, place a '#' before a parameter to exclude it.)

UTC time
Local time
Pressure (mb)
Pressure (Pa)
Temperature (C)
Relative humidity (%)
Wind speed (m/s)
Wind speed (kt)
Wind direction (true deg)
Wind heading (true deg)
Wind heading (magnetic deg)
Dew point (C)
Mixing ratio (g/kg)
Density (kg/m3)

Altitude (m MSL)
Altitude (ft MSL)
Height (m AGL)
Height (ft AGL)
GPS altitude (m MSL)
Internal temperature (C)
Latitude
Longitude
Uncorrected temperature (C)
Uncorrected humidity (%)

######################################################################
# These parameters are only available when use_actual_reports is enabled,
# since they are not interpolated for any given altitude:
Time from launch (sec)
Index
Unfiltered wind speed (m/s)
Unfiltered wind direction (true deg)
Unfiltered wind heading (true deg)
Unfiltered wind heading (magnetic deg)
Reception quality (%)
Rise speed (m/s)
Light level (lux)
